section_name: Surrender of Driver’s License or Personal Identification Certificate
text:
(a) A person is not entitled to receive a driver’s license until the person surrenders to the department each personal identification certificate in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.

(b) A person is not entitled to receive a personal identification certificate until the person surrenders to the department each driver’s license in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.183 — Surrender of Driver’s License or Personal Identification Certificate

(a) A person is not entitled to receive a driver’s license until the person surrenders to the department each personal identification certificate in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.

(b) A person is not entitled to receive a personal identification certificate until the person surrenders to the department each driver’s license in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.

*Added by Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1280 (S.B.1934), Sec. 5, eff. September 1, 2015.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A person is not entitled to receive a driver’s license until the person surrenders to the department each personal identification certificate in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A person is not entitled to receive a personal identification certificate until the person surrenders to the department each driver’s license in the person’s possession that was issued by this state.

history.raw: Added by Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 1280 (S.B.1934), Sec. 5, eff. September 1, 2015.
